tagline: Supercharge your teams with AI that gets work done
subheadline: Give your teams AI that understands their work, keeps projects moving, and gets better the more your teams use it.
value_proposition: An AI-powered work management platform that helps teams plan, track, and automate projects while connecting individual tasks to company-wide goals.
target_audience: Enterprise and mid-market teams across marketing, operations, IT, and leadership — particularly companies looking to integrate AI into existing workflows

testimonials: Asana makes everything shareable, which saves us time executing programs because it's so much easier to replicate and learn from what others have done. — Simon Levinson, Danone
To hit our ambitious growth target, we need to move fast. But we can't do it without clear processes that remove as many blockers as possible. — Romain Ehrhard, KENDO
Asana is our air traffic control. We have the visibility to scale production and support a higher number of ad campaigns to support our revenue goals. — Eugenia Contreras, Spotify
Having one centralized ecosystem that brings all doers and stakeholders together has really accelerated our understanding of the work we do. — Steven Tai, Gannett
press: Forrester Wave: Collaborative Work Management Tools 2025 — Leader
Gartner Magic Quadrant for Collaborative Work Management 2025 — Leader (three years in a row)

pricing.model: freemium with self-serve and contact-sales tiers
pricing.price_anchoring: Free personal tier lowers barrier to entry; enterprise tier requires sales contact, implying high value; analyst recognition (Forrester, Gartner) used to justify premium positioning

features: AI Teammates — autonomous AI agents that handle work end-to-end
AI Studio — build and configure AI workflows
Goals and reporting — connect team work to company-level OKRs
Workflows and automation — standardize and automate cross-department processes
Resource management — visualize team capacity and staffing
differentiators: AI that 'understands their work' and 'gets better the more your teams use it' — implying contextual, compounding intelligence
'The only platform that can support your company at any scale'
Asana Gov — FedRAMP-compliant offering for government agencies
Human + AI collaboration framing — positions AI as teammate, not just tool
trust_signals: Gartner Magic Quadrant Leader 2025 — three consecutive years
Asana Gov — compliance-grade product for government
Trust and security page linked in footer
CPRA/privacy compliance signals in cookie consent UI

design_patterns.layout: Full-width hero with centered headline, tabbed use-case section, horizontal scrolling carousel for case studies, three-column feature grid, analyst badge row, footer with extensive site map
design_patterns.color_scheme: Coral/red-pink primary brand color against white backgrounds; dark navy for text; muted grays for secondary content
design_patterns.typography: Clean, modern sans-serif; large bold hero headlines; medium-weight subtext; small caps used for section labels
design_patterns.visual_style: Minimal and professional with aspirational AI-forward messaging; warm but enterprise-grade
notable_patterns: Tab-switching hero section segmented by team role (Marketing, Operations, IT, Leadership)
Testimonial carousel with company size and industry metadata
Cookie consent modal with granular privacy controls

emotional_tone: Aspirational and confident — positions AI as an amplifier of human potential rather than a replacement; enterprise-authoritative without being cold
key_objections_addressed: Scale concerns — 'the only platform that can support your company at any scale'
Security/compliance for regulated industries — Asana Gov callout
AI adoption anxiety — framed as 'human + AI collaboration', not automation replacing people
Switching cost hesitation — 300+ integrations with existing enterprise tools
Complexity — 'Get started easily' section with demo, templates, and help articles
missing_or_weak: No visible pricing on the homepage — users must navigate to a separate pricing page, adding friction
Testimonials lack quantified outcomes (e.g., '40% faster campaign delivery') — qualitative only
AI differentiation is vague — 'gets better the more your teams use it' is not explained mechanically
No free trial CTA with a time bound (e.g., '30-day free trial') — 'Get started' is ambiguous
Social proof logos are limited to four named companies; no logo wall of recognizable brands
No video embed or product walkthrough in the hero — demo is gated behind a click
Competitor comparison or 'why switch from X' content is absent
standout_elements: Role-based hero tabs (Marketing/Operations/IT/Leadership) create immediate relevance segmentation
Analyst trifecta (Forrester + Gartner + G2) displayed together is unusually strong third-party validation
Asana Gov differentiates meaningfully for a large, underserved enterprise segment
'Human + AI collaboration' platform framing is resonant and timely without being hyperbolic
85% of Fortune 100 stat is a powerful, specific credibility anchor placed early in the page
